heroHeadline: Trump Envoy Proposes Iran→Italy World Cup Swap — Italy Calls It 'Shameful,' Italy Rejects It; Iran Sends Contradictory Signals With 7 Days to Vancouver FIFA Congress
heroSubtitle: Trump envoy Paolo Zampolli formally proposed to both Trump and FIFA President Infantino that Iran be replaced in the World Cup by Italy (which failed to qualify for a third straight time). Italy's government rejected it immediately — Sports Minister Abodi said 'firstly it is not possible, secondly it is not appropriate — you qualify on the pitch' and Economy Minister Giorgetti called it 'shameful.' Under FIFA regulations, the replacement slot would go to UAE, not Italy. Iran meanwhile sent contradictory signals: government spokesperson Fatemeh Mohajerani said Iran is 'fully prepared for effective participation,' while Iranian NOC Secretary General Mahdi Alinejad cautioned at the Asian Beach Games in Sanya that 'it is not my decision, it is the government's' and rejected Infantino's assurance that Iran would attend 'for sure.' Iran Sports Minister Donyamali had said April 21 'maybe the decision is not to go.' Separately, Lamine Yamal suffered a hamstring tear for Barcelona — he'll miss the rest of the club season but is expected to recover in time for Spain's June 15 opener. The April 30 FIFA Congress in Vancouver — 7 days away — remains the formal decision point on Iran's participation. 49 days to kickoff.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth, Fox 5 New York, Inside World Football, CBC Sports, Johnson County Post, PR Newswire, Yahoo Sports, RTE Sport, TRT World, Time, Xinhua
